issue.title: Heartbeat refill lane 1396: remaining Python asset sweep 6/10

commands: find src/bigclaw tests scripts bigclaw-go/scripts -type f -name '*.py' 2>/dev/null | sort

blocker.summary: The repository-wide physical Python file count was already zero in this workspace before BIG-GO-1396 changes.

notes: This lane documents an empty remaining Python asset inventory and hardens the Go-only state with regression coverage.
The branch baseline was already Python-free, so this lane strengthens regression coverage and refreshes validation evidence rather than removing in-branch Python files.
Baseline HEAD before lane changes was `c8e9d79c`.
A direct push from `4b179a97` to `origin/main` was rejected after the remote advanced; that validated tip remains published on `origin/big-go-1396`.
The lane was then rebased onto fetched `origin/main` base `8e561c4f`, producing local HEAD `92e371e2` and a fresh targeted regression pass in `0.486s`.
A second direct push from metadata-refreshed HEAD `bbf6f322` was rejected after `origin/main` advanced again to `19afd942`.
The full two-commit lane stack was then replayed onto fetched `origin/main` base `19afd942`, producing local HEAD `c106acad` and a fresh targeted regression pass in `0.882s`.
A follow-up push attempt first failed with a transient GitHub TLS error and then lost the race to a further `origin/main` advance to `0d551a17`.
The full three-commit lane stack was then replayed onto fetched `origin/main` base `0d551a17`, producing local HEAD `a30424a9` and a fresh targeted regression pass in `0.772s`.
When `origin/main` advanced again to `9fe3afa8`, the full four-commit lane stack was replayed onto that base, producing local HEAD `b8a6f0fe` and a fresh targeted regression pass in `3.207s`.
Metadata refresh commit `6c7c4b0d` was then pushed successfully to `origin/main`.
